original: Fé er frænda róg / ok flæðar viti / ok grafseiðs gata.

translations.standard: Wealth is the strife of kinsmen / and the fire of the flood / and the path of the serpent.
translations.literal: Wealth is kinsmen's strife / and flood's beacon [gold] / and grave-serpent's path.

original: Þurs er kvenna kvöl / ok kletta búi / ok varðrúnar verr.

translations.standard: Giant is the torture of women / and the cliff-dweller / and the husband of a giantess.
translations.literal: Giant is women's torment / and cliff-dweller / and ward-rune's [giantess's] man.

original: Óss er aldin-gautr / ok Ásgarðs jöfurr / ok Valhallar vísi.

translations.standard: God is the aged Gautr [Odin] / and the prince of Asgard / and the lord of Valhall.
translations.literal: God is old-Gautr / and Asgard's prince / and Valhall's leader.

original: Reið er sitjandi sæla / ok snörp ferð / ok jórs erfiði.

translations.standard: Riding is a sitting comfort / and a swift journey / and the toil of the horse.
translations.literal: Riding is sitting happiness / and sharp journey / and horse's toil.

original: Kaun er barna böl / ok bardaga för / ok húsasóttar hlutr.

translations.standard: Ulcer is the grief of children / and a place of battle / and the path of the pestilence.
translations.literal: Sore is children's bane / and battle's-way / and house-sickness's part.

original: Hagall er kaldakorn / ok krapadrífa / ok snotra sótt.

translations.standard: Hail is a cold grain / and a shower of sleet / and the sickness of serpents.
translations.literal: Hail is cold-grain / and sleet-drift / and snakes' sickness.

original: Nauð er þýjar þrá / ok þungr kostr / ok vássamlig verk.

translations.standard: Constraint is the grief of a bond-woman / and a hard condition / and toilsome work.
translations.literal: Need is bond-woman's longing / and heavy choice [condition] / and wet [toilsome] work.

original: Íss er árbörkr / ok fjarðar þak / ok feigra manna fár.

translations.standard: Ice is the bark of rivers / and the roof of the waves / and a danger for fey men.
translations.literal: Ice is river-bark / and fjord's roof / and fey [doomed] men's danger.

original: Ár er gumna góði / ok gott sumar / ok algróinn akr.

translations.standard: Plenty is the boon of men / and a good summer / and a well-grown field.
translations.literal: Year [Harvest] is men's benefit / and good summer / and all-grown field.

original: Sól er skýja skjöldr / ok skínandi röðull / ok ísa aldrtregi.

translations.standard: Sun is the shield of the clouds / and a shining ray / and the grief of ice.
translations.literal: Sun is clouds' shield / and shining halo / and ice's life-sorrow [destroyer].

original: Týr er einhendr áss / ok úlfs leifar / ok hofa hilmir.

translations.standard: Tyr is the one-handed god / and the leavings of the wolf / and the ruler of temples.
translations.literal: Tyr is one-handed god / and wolf's leavings / and temples' ruler.

original: Bjarkan er laufgrænt lík / ok lítit tré / ok unga-viðr.

translations.standard: Birch is a leafy-green limb / and a little tree / and a youthful wood.
translations.literal: Birch is leafy-green body / and little tree / and young-wood.

original: Maðr er moldar auki / ok skipa skreytir / ok nadda niðr.

translations.standard: Man is the increase of dust / and the adorner of ships / and the descendant of nails [warrior].
translations.literal: Man is dust's increase / and ships' adorner / and points' [nails/spears] descendant.

original: Lögr er vellanda vatn / ok viðr ketill / ok glamma hringr.

translations.standard: Water is a welling stream / and a wide cauldron / and the ring of the giants.
translations.literal: Water is welling water / and wide kettle / and giants' ring [the sea].

original: Ýr er beygðr bogi / ok hveiti-járn / ok fífu fár.

translations.standard: Yew is a bent bow / and brittle-iron / and the danger of the arrow.
translations.literal: Yew is bent bow / and wheat-iron [brittle iron] / and arrow's danger.
